rulings.Case Title: KURO VENTURES, LLC VS. ONE RINCON DEVELOPMENT, LLC (A.K.A. ONE RINCON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Arb
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 2, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT ONE RINCON DEVELOPMENT, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration. The motion to compel arbitration is granted. The parties agreed to arbitrate this dispute in their purchase agreement and also by stipulation. The attorney's fees clause is enforceable because this action does not involve a "consumer arbitration" within the meaning of CCP 1284.3. In addition, Kuro Venture's reliance on CCP 1284.3 and CCP 1281.2(c) to avoid arbitration runs afoul of the FAA and is preempted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. METRO CAB LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery For The Limited Purpose Of Taking The Depiosition Of Adel Tuwarish
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Tuesday, July 2, 2013, Line 1: Defendants METRO CAB LLC and RICHARD HYBELS' Motion to Reopen Discovery for the Limited Purpose of Taking the Deposition of Adel Tuwarish Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to reopen discovery for the limited purpose of taking the deposition of Mr. Adel Tuwarish is GRANTED. Plaintiff has obtained a declaration from a percipient witness who claims to have witnessed acts relevant to the issues raised by Plaintiff's complaint. The name of this witness was not previously disclosed in Plaintiff's responses to Form Interrogatory 12.1. Good cause exists to allow the deposition of this witness to go forward after the discovery cutoff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE CONNELL VS. HAR-BRO OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 2, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANT FERMA CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are each denied. Defendant Ferma Corporation failed to shift the burden of production of evidence on any cause of action. Regarding the sixteenth cause of action, Ferma failed to produce sufficient evidence to show there is no issue of material fact that it did not breach the Subcontract for two reasons. First, Plaintiff did not address each breach alleged by Plaintiff in the First Amended Complaint. Second, Ferma argues that it did not breach the Subcontract because it had no obligation to get a permit (SSUFs 9, 11) or tarp the building during the demolition process (SSUF 12). These arguments do not obviate Ferma's obligations as set forth in the Subcontract. (See Subcontract, Terms and Conditions, 1, 5, and 8.) There is thus an issue of material fact as to Ferma's obligations under the Subcontract. Regarding the third, fourth, fifth, sixth and seventh causes of action, these causes of action as pled do not arise simply from the breach of contract and involve duties "independent of the contract arising from principles of tort law." (Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 551.) Regarding the eighth cause of action, Ferma failed to show the absence of an issue of material fact as to whether it violated any statute or that the subject code violations are not designed to protect the Plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY BRYANT VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY DEPARTMENT OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 2, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court grants the motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff cannot establish a prima facie case for retaliation because there is no causal nexus between the protected activity and the adverse employment action. Plaintiff complained to defendant's HR department in February 2008 and the demotion occurred in February 2009. There is no inference of causation given the year long gap. There is no authority supporting the proposition that the time of the protected activity should be measured from when Defendant completed the investigation. Gov't § 12940 provides that a "complaint" is protected activity. In addition, it is not reasonable to infer that the decisionmakers (Ms. Mahmoudi and Mr. Rohan) harbored a retaliatory animus. They were not the subjects of the February 2008 complaint. Lastly, defendant presents a legitimate, non-retaliatory reason for its conduct and plaintiff does not present substantial responsive evidence showing pretext. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: HORACE GALDAMEZ et al VS. DIPAK PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Dipak Patel And 505 Cypress Avenue, Llc Dba The Industrial Hotel'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Tuesday, July 2, 2013, Line 2: Defendants DIPAK PATEL and 505 CYPRESS AVENUE, LLC dba THE INDUSTRIAL HOTEL'S Motion to Dismiss for Failure to Comply with Discovery Order Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion is GRANTED. Good cause exists to grant terminating sanctions with respect to plaintiffs Micles, Travis, Romes, Greider, and Abdalla due to their repeated failure to submit to deposition, and their disobedience of a court order compelling them to submit to deposition before May 31, 2013. The declaration testimony of Defendants' counsel Kevin P. Cody establishes that Defendants noticed the depositions of plaintiffs Cindy Mickles, Patricia Travis, Jamal Romes, Rachel Greider, and Derek Abdalla for November and December of 2012. Plaintiffs failed to attend their depositions. Defendants' counsel noticed plaintiffs' depositions again in April of 2013 for mid-May of 2013. Plaintiffs again failed to appear. Defense counsel obtained an order on May 17, 2013 compelling plaintiffs to submit to deposition on or before May 31, 2013. Plaintiffs have failed to do so. Trial is set for July 22, 2013. The complaints of plaintiffs Cindy Mickles, Patricia Travis, Jamal Romes, Rachel Greider, and Derek Abdalla ONLY are dismissed without prejudice. This order does not purport to affect the complaint of any other plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRANIK KARAM VS. LYNDON Y. CHEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 2, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANTs LYNDON CHEE and GEONETTA & FRUCHT LLP's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants' Lyndon Chee and Geonetta & Frucht special motion to strike is granted. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants satisfied the first prong under CCP § 425.16, as Plaintiff Karam's complaint arises from Defendants' written or oral statements made before a judicial proceeding. See CCP § 425.16(e)(1). Moreover, "every Court of Appeal that has addressed the question has concluded that malicious prosecution causes of action fall within the purview of the anti-SLAPP statute." Jarrow Formulas, Inc. v. LaMarche, 31 Cal.4th 728, 735 (2003). Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the element of probable cause. Specifically, Plaintiff has failed show prima facie facts that all reasonable lawyers would agree that Defendant Jacobs' previous claim totally lacked merit. Jarrow, 31 Cal.4th at 743, fn. 13. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: GESTAMP ASETYM SOLAR NORTH AMERICA, INC. VS. GCL SOLAR SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 2, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTs GCL SOLAR SOLUTIONS, INC. & GCL SOLAR ENERGY, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion is continued to July 31, 2013, to give the moving party an opportunity to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall provide a courtesy copy of the reply with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date by July 10, 2013. The Court has the moving and opposing papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/RAK)
